decks.notes: This Pauper 1 vs 1 Golgari/Jund Garden control deck focuses on utilizing various sacrifice effects and removal spells to control the board and outvalue opponents. The deck includes cards like Crypt Rats, Defile, and Nihil Spellbomb to control the board, while also incorporating card draw and resource generation with cards like Ichor Wellspring and Lembas. Additionally, the deck includes powerful finishers like Thorn of the Black Rose and Avenging Hunter to close out games. The strategy revolves around utilizing efficient removal and sacrifice effects to gain incremental advantages and outvalue opponents.

Crafted around the Dimir archetype, this Pauper Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while gaining card advantage and protecting its own threats. With a mix of efficient counterspells, removal spells, and evasive creatures, the deck aims to control the board and outvalue the opponent. Faerie Seer and Spellstutter Sprite provide early aggression, while Augur of Bolas, Ninja of the Deep Hours, and Thorn of the Black Rose offer card advantage. With a well-balanced mix of disruption and card advantage, this Dimir Control deck aims to outsmart and outlast its opponents in the Pauper 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on playing low-cost artifacts to quickly create a board presence and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures. By utilizing cards that can bounce artifacts back to hand, as well as cards that generate extra artifacts, the deck aims to constantly have threats on the board. The inclusion of various artifact lands and artifact-focused spells allows for efficient mana production and card draw. Overall, the strategy revolves around combining affinity for artifacts with aggressive plays to establish dominance on the battlefield.

The main concept of this Pauper 1 vs 1 deck is to efficiently use the Tireless Tribe combo to buff up the creature and deal massive damage to the opponent. Cards like Sneaky Snacker and Spirit Mascot provide support for the combo, while spells like Inside Out and Fling are used to enhance the Tribe's power. The deck also includes cards for card draw and mana fixing, as well as protection spells like Prismatic Strands. Overall, the deck's goal is to quickly set up and execute the Tireless Tribe combo to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

This Pauper Jund Aggro deck focuses on using efficient creature removal and disruption spells to control the board while pumping out threats like the Nyxborn Hydra and Krark-Clan Shaman. The deck also utilizes recursion with cards like Refurbished Familiar and pulse of Murasa to maintain board presence and grind out wins. The inclusion of artifacts like Blood Fountain and Drossforge Bridge adds additional value and synergies with the deck's overall strategy. With a mix of aggression and resilience, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through consistent pressure and resource management.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively attack the opponent with low-cost creatures and burn spells to quickly deplete their life total. By efficiently using direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast, combined with creatures like Voldaren Epicure and Sneaky Snacker to apply pressure, the deck aims to end the game as quickly as possible. The inclusion of cards like Faithless Looting and Grab the Prize help with card advantage and mana efficiency, allowing the deck to maintain momentum and keep the pressure on the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with cheap aggressive creatures and burn spells, aiming to deal direct damage to the opponent and their creatures. By combining efficient creatures with direct burn spells, the deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while also providing ways to generate card advantage and steal resources. The deck is designed to be fast-paced and aggressive, aiming to win the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize or mount a strong defense.

This Red Deck Wins Pauper deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing cards like Goblin Bushwhacker and Burning-Tree Emissary to quickly establish a board presence, coupled with direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Blast, the deck aims to deal quick and efficient damage to the opponent. Rally at the Hornburg provides additional reach and can help close out games. The deck also includes some card draw and card advantage options to ensure consistent pressure on the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost artifact creatures and using various artifact synergies to overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive playstyle. The deck aims to generate advantage through card draw and efficient creature recursion, while also utilizing removal spells and utility cards to maintain board control. By consistently applying pressure and leveraging the deck's affinity for artifacts, players can quickly close out games and outpace their opponents in the Pauper format.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan while also utilizing various card draw and filtering spells to maintain card advantage. The deck relies on utilizing its counterspells, removal spells, and efficient creatures like Gurmag Angler to control the board and limit the opponent's options. The inclusion of cards like Tolarian Terror and Lórien Revealed help provide additional card advantage and flexibility in the deck's strategy. The deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent by efficiently using its resources to control the game.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with self-mill cards like Satyr Wayfinder and Stinkweed Imp, then bringing back powerful creatures like Lotleth Giant and Molten Gatekeeper using Dread Return. The combo relies on generating value through reanimation and sacrificing creatures with cards like Generous Ent and Malevolent Rumble. Town Greeter helps with card draw and Mortuary Mire can bring back key creatures from the graveyard. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big creatures and efficient reanimation tactics.

This deck focuses on deploying low-cost artifacts quickly using cards like Refurbished Familiar and Myr Enforcer to overwhelm the opponent with strong attacking creatures. Krark-Clan Shaman provides additional damage while Blood Fountain and Nihil Spellbomb disrupt the opponent's graveyard strategies. Thoughtcast and Reckoner's Bargain help draw more cards to keep the aggression going. Galvanic Blast serves as direct damage and removal, while Ichor Wellspring provides card advantage. The inclusion of various artifact lands and mana sources ensures consistent mana production for a fast and aggressive playstyle.

This Jund Aggro deck focuses on card advantage and disruption to control the board while applying pressure with efficient creatures and removal spells. The deck aims to use cards like Refurbished Familiar and Ichor Wellspring to draw cards and generate value, while also using spells like Cast Down and Cleansing Wildfire to disrupt opponent's strategy. Krark-Clan Shaman provides additional removal and Lembas offers lifegain to stabilize against aggressive opponents. Overall, the deck aims to maintain a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's strategy to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent using cheap and efficient creatures and burn spells. By maximizing the number of aggressive creatures and direct damage spells, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Faithless Looting and Grab the Prize helps to maintain card advantage and consistency, ensuring a steady stream of threats and burn spells. With a solid mana base of Mountains, this deck is designed to put pressure on the opponent from the very beginning of the game.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Goblin Bushwhacker and Voldaren Epicure, along with efficient burn spells like Galvanic Blast and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to deal substantial damage early in the game. The inclusion of cards like Clockwork Percussionist and Experimental Synthesizer allows for additional damage and card advantage. The deck's strategy is to maintain pressure on the opponent, rapidly depleting their life total before they have a chance to stabilize.

This white aggro deck focuses on flooding the board with low-cost creatures and token generators to overwhelm opponents quickly. Including a mix of creatures with evasion and buffs spells, the deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain a steady stream of threats. The inclusion of cards like Thraben Charm and Prismatic Strands adds versatility and protection to the strategy. By consistently presenting a wide board presence, this deck aims to outmuscle opponents in the early game and secure victory through sheer aggression.
